what_it_does: Implements a client-side AI synthesis driver exposed as window.MS_SYNTH_AI that calls an LLM via window.claude.complete to summarize source material, generate project-specific design directions, and draft an ordered sequence spine, with defensive JSON parsing and null fallbacks when the LLM is unavailable.
observations: Uses an IIFE module pattern attached to window.MS_SYNTH_AI.
Relies on a global window.claude.complete API for LLM calls.
Prompts adopt a 'Vi' persona and request strict compact JSON output.
Includes a custom salvageJson helper that attempts to repair truncated JSON responses.
Deterministic paletteFor maps renderer tags to warm-tone values client-side to save tokens.
Looks up approved/style-anchor mockups from window.MS_DATA.mockups for anchor linking.
Injects a HOUSE visual-language constant into LLM prompts.
Optionally appends feedback learnings from window.MS_FEEDBACK into prompts.
Enforces that exactly one generated direction is marked recommended.
Sequence output includes a suggested title, kickoff steps, and a cost/latency estimate schema.
standouts: The salvageJson truncation-repair logic with depth tracking and dangling-comma cleanup.
The anchor-catalog lookup that ties generated directions back to approved mockups.
The defensive null-fallback pattern when the LLM is unreachable or returns bad JSON.
conventions: Async functions with try/catch and null fallback on failure.
Template-literal prompt construction with embedded JSON.stringify of brief fields.
Defensive output normalization after LLM calls.
Global dependency access through window.* objects.
Snake_case IDs for directions and renderer tags.
concerns: Depends on globals window.claude, window.MS_DATA, and window.MS_FEEDBACK without local interfaces.
salvageJson uses a depth<=2 heuristic that may close structures incorrectly in nested cases.
Prompts embed arbitrary brief JSON directly, which may bloat context or leak formatting.
No schema validation beyond checking arrays and presence of fields.
Cost/latency estimate schema is hardcoded in the prompt rather than derived.
No visible caching, rate-limiting, or retry logic for LLM calls.
notes: This file is the live synthesis bridge: it shapes raw brief/source material into Vi-generated directions and a sequence spine, while doing its own JSON cleanup when the model response is messy.
questions: What is the exact contract of window.claude.complete and which model does it use?
How are source contents actually extracted when only names and providers are sent to the LLM?
Where and how is window.MS_DATA.mockups populated?
What happens downstream when summarizeSources, directions, or sequence return null?
Is the renderer taxonomy defined elsewhere, and what does each renderer imply visually?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose in the header matches the implementation: it provides live AI synthesis of summaries, directions, and sequences.
intention_signals: The file is trying to turn a design brief and attached material into a conversational design partner (Vi) that proposes tailored creative directions and a narrative spine, while staying usable even when the LLM misbehaves or is offline.
novelty: The custom JSON truncation salvager and the deterministic renderer-to-mood-palette mapping are unusual; linking generated directions to approved mockup anchors is also distinctive.
contribution: Provides a reusable pattern for structuring LLM prompts, repairing malformed JSON responses, and mapping LLM output into project-specific direction and sequence objects; contributes the salvageJson repair heuristic and renderer-based palette generation.
summary_for_embedding: A client-side AI synthesis driver that invokes an LLM to summarize sources, propose design directions, and draft experience sequences, with defensive JSON parsing and fallback handling.
intention_for_embedding: To give Counterpart a live, Vi-powered synthesis layer that turns briefs and source material into structured design proposals and narrative spines.

symbols.does: Strips markdown fences and parses a JSON string, falling back to salvageJson on failure.
symbols.description: This function cleans LLM output by removing leading/trailing code fences, locating the first object, and attempting JSON.parse; if parsing fails it delegates to salvageJson to recover from truncation.

symbols.does: Repairs truncated JSON by tracking depth and strings, then closes open structures.
symbols.description: It walks the string to find the last safe completed element, strips trailing commas, and closes any remaining arrays or objects so that a partial LLM response can still be parsed.

symbols.does: Checks whether the global LLM completion API is present.
symbols.description: Returns true if window.claude and window.claude.complete exist, used to decide whether to attempt LLM calls or return null.

symbols.does: Collects approved or style-anchor mockups from window.MS_DATA.mockups.
symbols.description: Filters the global mockups list to entries with status 'approved' or 'style_anchor' and returns a compact catalog of id, title, src, and note for prompt context.

symbols.does: Returns the image source for a given mockup id.
symbols.description: Looks up a mockup by id in window.MS_DATA.mockups and returns its src property, used to attach anchor images to generated directions.

symbols.does: Formats the brief's readable sources as a markdown list.
symbols.description: Filters sources marked for reading and builds a concise list of name, provider, and kind for inclusion in LLM prompts.

symbols.does: Calls the LLM to summarize provided source material and infer brief facts.
symbols.description: Builds a Vi prompt from the brief and reading list, requests JSON with summary, inferred fields, and facts, then normalizes the response.

symbols.does: Computes a deterministic mood palette from a direction's renderer and index.
symbols.description: Maps renderer tags to fixed warm-tone values, attaches the gold accent color, and copies up to three mood words, avoiding another LLM call for palette generation.

symbols.does: Calls the LLM to generate four distinct design directions for the project.
symbols.description: Prompts Vi with the brief, material, house style, and optional feedback learnings, then normalizes the returned directions, validates renderer IDs, attaches palettes and anchors, and ensures one direction is recommended.

symbols.does: Calls the LLM to draft an ordered sequence spine for the selected direction.
symbols.description: Prompts Vi with the goal, audience, chosen direction, and renderer label, then normalizes the returned states, title, kickoff steps, and estimate object.
